title: Humidity Monitoring
spl:
index=environment sourcetype="sensor:humidity"
| where humidity_pct > 60 OR humidity_pct < 40
| table _time, zone, humidity_pct
description: Low humidity causes ESD risk; high humidity causes condensation. Maintaining 40-60% RH protects equipment.
value: Low humidity causes ESD risk; high humidity causes condensation. Maintaining 40-60% RH protects equipment.
implementation: Deploy humidity sensors alongside temperature sensors. Alert on out-of-range humidity (below 40% or above 60% RH). Track dew point to prevent condensation. Correlate with HVAC system humidifier/dehumidifier operation.
visualization: Line chart (humidity trend), Gauge (current humidity per zone), Table (zones out of range).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: SNMP environmental sensors.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Humidity sensors.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Deploy humidity sensors alongside temperature sensors. Alert on out-of-range humidity (below 40% or above 60% RH). Track dew point to prevent condensation. Correlate with HVAC system humidifier/dehumidifier operation.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=environment sourcetype="sensor:humidity"
| where humidity_pct > 60 OR humidity_pct < 40
| table _time, zone, humidity_pct
```

Understanding this SPL

**Humidity Monitoring** — Low humidity causes ESD risk; high humidity causes condensation. Maintaining 40-60% RH protects equipment.

Documented **Data sources**: Humidity sensors. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): SNMP environmental sensors. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: environment; **sourcetype**: sensor:humidity. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=environment, sourcetype="sensor:humidity". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where humidity_pct > 60 OR humidity_pct < 40` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Humidity Monitoring**): table _time, zone, humidity_pct


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the BMS console for the same equipment, sensors, and time range.


Step 4 — Operationalize

Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (humidity trend), Gauge (current humidity per zone), Table (zones out of range).
grandmaExplanation: We watch the physical data center for power, cooling, environment, and physical access.
knownFalsePositives: Sensor anomalies during cleaning, calibration, or planned environmental changes.
